commit.message:
feat(response): add NoContent, BadRequest, Unauthorized, Forbidden, NotFound, Conflict, UnprocessableEntity, Accepted helpers

Add eight convenience wrappers around Error/JSON for the most
common HTTP status codes. Callers no longer need to import
net/http status constants just to send a standard error response.

- Accepted(w, data)            → 202
- NoContent(w)                 → 204 (no body)
- BadRequest(w, msg)           → 400
- Unauthorized(w, msg)         → 401
- Forbidden(w, msg)            → 403
- NotFound(w, msg)             → 404
- Conflict(w, msg)             → 409
- UnprocessableEntity(w, msg)  → 422

Tests and Example tests added; coverage remains 100%.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: switch from custom KQL tools to built-in platform tools

Replace 6 custom KustoTool YAMLs with built-in SRE Agent platform tools:
- Redis: 7 dedicated tools (GetRedisHitMissRatio, GetRedisMemoryUsage, etc.)
- Azure Monitor: AnalyzeMetrics, DiscoverMetrics for ServiceBus/SQL/CosmosDB
- AKS: RunAzCliReadCommands for kubectl via az aks command invoke
- Health: GetResourceHealth, SearchResource for resource discovery

The custom KQL tools required a Log Analytics Kusto connector which has a
platform limitation with ADE proxy URLs (KustoConnector.cs uses Uri.Host
stripping the workspace path). Built-in tools work without connectors.

commit.message:
feat: add master meta-strategy cycling 19 sub-strategies across 3 tiers

Architecture:
  PerformanceMonitor  — tracks win/loss streaks, peak drawdown, session drawdown
  StrategyPool        — lazy-instantiates sub-strategies with private deques
  SwitchEngine        — evaluates triggers and advances round-robin within tiers
  MasterStrategy      — @register('master') orchestrator, schema-driven

Tier layout (all use EV-optimised defaults):
  Tier 0 SAFE       (12): unified-progression, oscars-grind, fib-loss-cluster,
                          unified-martingale, target-aware, unified-faucet,
                          kelly-capped, paroli, rng-analysis-strategy,
                          one-three-two-six, chance-descent, adaptive-survival
  Tier 1 MODERATE   (5):  luck-cascade, adaptive-hunter, chance-cycle-multiplier,
                          oracle-engine, simple-progression-40
  Tier 2 AGGRESSIVE (2):  profit-cascade, streak-multiplier

Switching triggers (defaults):
  rotation_interval=75      → rotate within tier (round-robin)
  loss_streak_rotate=8      → rotate within tier (skip current)
  loss_streak_deescalate=15 → drop one tier
  win_streak_escalate=12    → raise one tier
  drawdown_deescalate=8%    → drop one tier
  drawdown_emergency=20%    → force tier 0
  allow_aggressive=True     → cap at tier 2 (False = cap at MODERATE)

perf: switch flash reads to MSP v2, bump baud to 921600

  enabling ~4 KB responses instead of 255 B with v1 (~16x per-frame gain)

- Flash chunk size: 16384 → 4096 (matches Betaflight's serial buffer)
- Retry delay: 100ms → 10ms for faster error recovery
- Estimated 16 MB sync time: ~20 min → ~2.5 min (UART), ~1 min (USB)

commit.message:
fix: resolve identity/middleware context key incompatibility (#31)

identity.GetUserID() and identity.GetExtCandidateID() used a private
contextKey type (identity.contextKey) that was incompatible with the
contextKey set by middleware.SetUserID() (middleware.contextKey). Since
Go context key lookup compares both type and value, identity.ResolveCandidate()
would always see an empty user ID and never resolve via DB lookup.

Fix: identity.GetUserID and identity.GetExtCandidateID now delegate to
middleware.GetUserID and middleware.GetExtCandidateID, ensuring they
read the same context keys that JWT auth middleware writes.

Also adds middleware.SetExtCandidateID() to give extension token
middleware a proper setter function for the candidate ID context key.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: address PR #26 review feedback — regex boundaries, LRU cache, error tests

- telemetry: replace includes() with regex word-boundary patterns for
  status codes (401/403) and auth phrases to avoid false positives
  (e.g. '401k' no longer triggers error detection)
- telemetry: add tests verifying onToolComplete marks spans as ERROR
  for auth failures and as OK for non-error results containing '401'
- client: make session cache truly LRU — refresh Map position on cache
  hits via delete+re-set so frequently used sessions are not evicted

colorbleed: diagnostic XML fallback for malformed ICC profiles

When iccDEV's CIccProfile::Read() fails on malformed profiles (e.g., P0 PoCs),
iccToXml_unsafe now generates a diagnostic XML from raw binary analysis instead
of returning an empty error. The diagnostic XML includes:

- Full header field decode (version, class, color space, PCS, illuminant, etc.)
- Tag table with type signatures, hex previews, and ASCII interpretation
- Anomaly detection (invalid magic, size mismatch, fuzz artifacts, OOB tags)
- Raw header hex dump for forensic analysis

New file: ColorBleedDiagnosticXml.h — self-contained diagnostic XML generator
using only raw file I/O (no iccDEV library calls). Defensively caps all
iterations, validates all offsets against file size, XML-escapes all output.

Exit code 6 = diagnostic mode (distinct from exit 0 clean, 2 read error).

Tested: P0-2225 (504B, 3597B XML), P0-2226 (7460B, 6054B XML), sRGB (clean
exit 0, 81872B normal XML). All outputs pass xmllint --noout.

refactor: Replace Entity Framework Core with Dapper ORM

  * GetOverdueAircraftAsync now uses SqlConnection with Dapper extensions
  * Direct SQL for aircraft filtering with case-insensitive LIKE searches
  * Separate queries for aircraft and compliance logs for clarity

  * Removed EF Core dependency injection via AppDbContext
  * Direct SQL queries with QuerySingleAsync for record counts
  * Maintains connection string parsing and detailed logging

- All services now depend on IConfiguration for connection strings
- Reduces abstraction layer and improves query control/performance

- More explicit SQL control
- Reduced ORM overhead
- Simpler dependency injection (no DbContext)
- Better for SQL Server/Azure SQL focused applications
